name: Vieux Carré

spec.method_detail: Stir with ice until well-chilled. Strain over large rock in rocks glass.

history: Created by Walter Bergeron at the Carousel Bar in Hotel Monteleone in New Orleans, c. 1938. 'Vieux Carré' means 'Old Square' — the French Quarter of New Orleans. The drink encapsulates New Orleans: American rye, French cognac, Italian vermouth, and French-Belgian Bénédictine, seasoned with Angostura (Trinidad) and Peychaud's (New Orleans). A genuinely complex cocktail that rewards attention.
